metadata.name: Enable Docker TLS
metadata.description: Enables TLS on the Docker daemon using existing certificates. Configures Docker for secure remote API access over an encrypted connection.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
This playbook enables TLS on the Docker daemon using an existing certificate set.

## Prerequisites
- Generate the Docker TLS certificates first.
- Confirm the certificate paths on the target host match the rendered playbook values.

## Run
1. Render `main.yml` and review the certificate path.
2. Run the playbook against the Docker workload hosts that should expose a TLS-protected API. The workload inventory already provides the host selection, so the playbook runs against `all` selected hosts.
3. Reconnect Docker clients with the matching CA and client certificates after the daemon restarts.

## Docs
- [Docker TLS Documentation](https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/protect-access/)
